title: Cambridge, MA bicycle crash of June 23, 2016

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Cambridge, MA bicycle crash of June 23, 2016
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Background information on the State Police investigative report in case # 2016-CAR-00179, a fatal bicycle crash in Inman Square, Cambridge, Massachusetts on June 23, 2016, and specifically:

* Any statements, notes or other materials supplied by Primary Investigating Officer Jerry Jean-Philippe of the City of Cambridge Police.
* Any unpublished materials prepared by the Reconstructionist, Massachusetts State Trooper David C. Bergeron, #3574.
* All photos taken at the scene by Trooper Karrol Setalsingh #1875 of the Massachusetts State Police Crime Scene Services Section, Boston, and any other photos taken in connection with the investigation.
* Report on examination of the landscaping truck(and trailer) involved in the incident, owned by Cambridge Landscaping Co., Inc., by Trooper Paul Powell #1747 of the Commercial Vehicle Enforcement Section Massachusetts State Police, including any photos and diagrams.
* Video clips from cameras 14 and camera 16 at the Inman Square Pharmacy at their original 640 x 480 pixel resolution, covering the entire time interval of the lower-resolution clips released to the public.

Statement(s) by
* The operator of the Jeep Cherokee, Massachusetts license 563 HB7, Male, Somerville resident, DOB 1969.
* the operator of the landscaping truck, male, Woburn, MA resident, DOB 1950.
* the passenger in the landscaping truck, male, Allston, MA resident, DOB 1970.
* the operator of MBTA bus #350, (female, DOB 1986).
* passenger on MBTA bus, female
* bystander witness (female)

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John S. Allen
